$schema_note: Documentation-only evidence artifact for task F0-010. This inventory does not authorize any queue, outbox, or worker implementation. It classifies the CURRENT (pre-implementation) shape of asynchronous and pseudo-asynchronous behavior in the repository, and proposes (does not decide) outbox/queue candidacy for a future, separately-authorized PoC and F6 implementation phase.
task: F0-010 — Queue and Transactional Outbox Proof-of-Concept Design
phase: F0 — Baseline, Program Control, and Architecture Validation

generated_from.baseline_pr: PR #177 merge commit (docs(architecture): design tenant RLS and PgBouncer PoC (F0-009))
generated_from.method: Targeted CodeGraph-equivalent (Glob/Grep/Read, no .codegraph/ index present in this worktree) investigation of server/src/jobs, server/src/routes (whatsapp.ts, instagramWebhook.ts, appointmentRequests.ts, communicationPreferences.ts, clinicBulkExport.ts, patientPrivacy.ts, imagingBridgePublic.ts, notifications.ts), server/src/services (messagingInboundIdempotency.ts, whatsapp/*, privacy/clinicBulkExportPackage.ts, operationalEventService.ts, auditLog.ts), server/src/utils/jobLock.ts, and server/prisma/schema.prisma. Two independent research passes cross-checked job/lock infrastructure and domain business flows.
generated_from.coverage_statement: BOUNDED, not repository-wide. This inventory covers every job file under server/src/jobs/ (9/9) and every domain flow named in the F0-010 task's 'at minimum inspect' list. It does NOT claim to have found every asynchronous or eventual-consistency code path in the repository (e.g. provider-level delivery-receipt webhooks were grepped but not exhaustively traced; the full imagingBridgePublic.ts studies-upload handler, 400+ lines, was not read in full). Any flow not listed here should be treated as UNCLASSIFIED, not as 'confirmed synchronous/safe'.

execution_mode_legend.synchronous_inline: DB write and side effect both happen inside the HTTP request/response cycle, before the response is sent
execution_mode_legend.response_first_fire_and_forget: HTTP response is sent first (e.g. webhook ack), then processing happens with no caller waiting and no automatic retry of the response path
execution_mode_legend.cron_polling: A node-cron schedule periodically sweeps a table and acts on matching rows
execution_mode_legend.queued_worker: A DB-row 'queued' status is claimed by a polling worker via a guarded atomic claim (updateMany), allowing multiple concurrent worker replicas

candidate_need_legend.YES: Repository evidence shows a concrete gap (lost update, crash-orphaned state, unretried external call) that an outbox/queue pattern would directly address
candidate_need_legend.NO: Either no gap was found, or the flow already has an equivalent internal solution (e.g. atomic upsert, guarded claim) that a general-purpose outbox/queue would not materially improve
candidate_need_legend.PARTIAL: A gap exists but a narrower fix (e.g. wrap two writes in one $transaction, add a DB unique constraint) would resolve it without a queue/outbox
candidate_need_legend.AMBIGUOUS: Evidence is insufficient in this bounded investigation to classify; flagged for a future targeted look, not a PoC input as-is

flows.name: WhatsApp inbound webhook processing — Evolution provider
flows.initiating_transaction: prisma.messagingInboundEvent.create() via createInboundEventOrDetectDuplicate (server/src/services/messagingInboundIdempotency.ts:53-73), called from whatsapp.ts:3679-3691 and :3773-3785
flows.side_effect: AI intent extraction, outbound WhatsApp reply send (whatsapp.ts:3728), WhatsAppConversationMessage persistence (:3729-3732) — all inline before the HTTP response
flows.tenant_key: resolveClinicForIncomingMessage priority chain: reply context, recent conversation state, single-clinic connection, else needsClinicResolution=true (server/src/services/whatsapp/clinicResolver.ts:75-160)
flows.retry_behavior: none — inboundEventRetryJob.ts explicitly excludes this provider (only channel='whatsapp', provider='meta_cloud_api' is retried; inboundEventRetryJob.ts:14-16, 53-54)
flows.idempotency_key: @@unique([channel, provider, connectionId, providerMessageId]) (schema.prisma:1610), enforced via Prisma P2002 catch
flows.ordering_requirement: soft — single mutable WhatsAppConversationState row per (clinicId, phone); no sequence number enforced
flows.delivery_guarantee_currently_achieved: at-least-once acceptance from the provider, but at-most-once processing with silent permanent loss on mid-processing failure (event lands in status='failed' and is never retried for this provider)
flows.failure_state: catch block marks event failed, returns HTTP 500 (whatsapp.ts:3820-3828); no operator-visible alert
flows.replay_capability: raw payload stored (rawPayload column) so a manual replay is technically possible; no admin tooling exists to trigger one
flows.audit_evidence: none — no AuditLog/OperationalEvent row on this path, console.error/warn only
flows.pii_health_data_sensitivity: HIGH — phone numbers, patient names, free-text message content including possible symptom/complaint text
flows.candidate_outbox_reasoning: This is an inbound acceptance path, not a flow that originates a committed DB transaction needing to publish an event — outbox solves 'reliably publish after commit', not 'reliably process an inbound webhook'.
flows.candidate_queue_reasoning: The gap is retry/dead-letter coverage, not delivery ordering or fan-out — extending inboundEventRetryJob.ts to cover this provider (or replacing it with a real queue consumer with backoff) would close the silent-loss gap without necessarily requiring an external queue product.
flows.migration_risk: MEDIUM — touches the WhatsApp AI/consent pipeline, one of the two domains (WHA) with the highest dependency fan-out (106 edges) in DEPENDENCY_MAP.md §10.3

flows.name: WhatsApp inbound webhook processing — Meta Cloud API provider
flows.initiating_transaction: Same createInboundEventOrDetectDuplicate path as F010-001, provider='meta_cloud_api'
flows.retry_behavior: YES — the only true attempt-counted retry mechanism in the backend: inboundEventRetryJob.ts, MAX_ATTEMPTS=3, RETRY_WINDOW_MS=6h, MIN_AGE_MS=5min fixed (not exponential) backoff, STUCK_PROCESSING_MS=1h crash-recovery sweep (inboundEventRetryJob.ts:29-33, 38-48)
flows.delivery_guarantee_currently_achieved: at-least-once, bounded to 3 attempts over 6 hours; permanently dropped (implicit dead-letter) after MAX_ATTEMPTS with no explicit poison-message status or alert
flows.failure_state: status='failed', attempts incremented; excluded from future sweeps once attempts >= 3 (inboundEventRetryJob.ts:55)
flows.replay_capability: raw payload stored; retry job itself is the replay mechanism up to 3 attempts; no manual replay tooling beyond that
flows.candidate_queue_reasoning: Already has a working, evidenced retry mechanism (JobLock-guarded cron sweep). A future queue could add exponential backoff and an explicit poison-message status/alert, but is not required to close a correctness gap the way it is for F010-001/F010-003.
flows.migration_risk: LOW — smallest incremental change of the three inbound-webhook rows since a retry mechanism already exists to extend

flows.name: Instagram inbound DM webhook processing
flows.initiating_transaction: prisma.messagingInboundEvent.create() via createInboundEventOrDetectDuplicate, called from processInstagramEventForConnection (instagramWebhook.ts:504-515)
flows.side_effect: upsertInstagramInboxEntry then conditional AI conversation processing (instagramWebhook.ts:533-558) — runs AFTER the HTTP response is already sent
flows.tenant_key: resolveClinicForInstagramMessage — inbox entry, single-clinic connection, connection default, single-org-clinic, else unresolved (instagramClinicResolver.ts:42-84); connection resolved from payload provider IDs, never the URL, by design (file header :5-6)
flows.retry_behavior: none — excluded from inboundEventRetryJob.ts same as Evolution (inboundEventRetryJob.ts:14-16)
flows.idempotency_key: Same shared unique constraint as F010-001/002; explicit duplicate check at instagramWebhook.ts:517-523
flows.ordering_requirement: low-moderate — similar single-row conversation-state risk as WhatsApp
flows.delivery_guarantee_currently_achieved: at-most-once processing with NO recovery path — res.sendStatus(200) is sent BEFORE parsing/processing (instagramWebhook.ts:389, :409), so Meta will never redeliver regardless of downstream outcome, and this provider is excluded from the internal retry job. This is the clearest silent-data-loss path found in this inventory.
flows.failure_state: markInboundEventFailed called, error swallowed by outer try/catch (instagramWebhook.ts:399-401, 436-438); no operator visibility beyond the DB row
flows.replay_capability: raw payload stored (rawPayload: asJsonRecord(event.rawPayload), :514); no tooling to trigger a replay
flows.audit_evidence: partial — writeAuditLog used only for connection-resolution failures/no-secret conditions (logWebhookEvent, :60-77), not for successful processing or markInboundEventFailed cases
flows.candidate_outbox_reasoning: Same reasoning as F010-001 — inbound acceptance, not a DB-transaction-originated event publication.
flows.candidate_queue_reasoning: Highest-priority queue candidate in this inventory: ack-before-process with zero retry coverage is the textbook case a durable queue/retry-consumer model exists to fix. Signature verification (HMAC-SHA256, instagramWebhook.ts:79-96) is already stronger than WhatsApp's, so the remaining gap is purely retry/durability, not authenticity.

flows.name: WhatsApp outbound appointment reminder send
flows.side_effect: Live external provider call via sendProactiveWhatsAppMessage (reminders.ts:282, whatsappOutboundMessaging.ts:288-329), then prisma.sentMessage.update({status:'sent'|'failed'}) (reminders.ts:313/331)
flows.tenant_key: clinic.id / clinic.organizationId from cron loop context, bounded concurrency CLINIC_CONCURRENCY=5 (reminders.ts:563-566, 573)
flows.retry_behavior: none — a failed send sets status='failed' and only logs to console (reminders.ts:330-336); no requeue, no dedicated retry job scans SentMessage
flows.idempotency_key: none (DB constraint) — application-level check-then-insert: findFirst on (clinicId, appointmentId, status in ['sent','delivered'], createdAt >= todayStart) (reminders.ts:257-265), a genuine race window mitigated only by the cron-tick-level JobLock, not a unique constraint
flows.ordering_requirement: low — each reminder independent per patient/appointment
flows.delivery_guarantee_currently_achieved: best-effort, 'sent' != 'delivered' — no production code path ever sets SentMessage.status to 'delivered'/'read' (confirmed by repository-wide grep; only a test fixture uses that literal). A crash between the provider call succeeding and the status='sent' update leaves the row stuck at 'prepared' with no reconciliation sweep (unlike MessagingInboundEvent's stuck-'processing' recovery in inboundEventRetryJob.ts:38-48).
flows.failure_state: per-patient try/catch, loop continues (reminders.ts:330-336); a stuck 'prepared' row is not resolved by any job
flows.replay_capability: none automated; alreadySent check only excludes status in ['sent','delivered'], so a stuck 'prepared' row could theoretically be re-attempted the next day, or never resolved, depending on the specific check
flows.audit_evidence: ActivityLog (not AuditLog) on success only (reminders.ts:318-329); nothing on failure
flows.pii_health_data_sensitivity: HIGH — patient name, phone, appointment details in message body
flows.candidate_outbox_reasoning: Textbook 'reliably execute an external side effect and record its true outcome' problem: the DB-write / external-call / status-update sequence is not atomic and has a demonstrated crash gap. A dispatcher pattern (claim -> call -> acknowledge) modeled on the existing clinicBulkExportWorker.ts precedent (F010-009) would close this.
flows.candidate_queue_reasoning: Retry-with-backoff for provider-call failures does not exist today and a queue/worker is the natural place to add it, consistent with F6's stated objective.
flows.migration_risk: MEDIUM-HIGH — reminders.ts is consent-gated (assertCommunicationPermission) and directly patient-facing; any change must preserve the existing consent-gate ordering and the distinct 'blocked_by_consent' outcome (reminders.ts:293-309)

flows.name: WhatsApp outbound payment installment reminder send
flows.initiating_transaction: Same pattern as F010-004, keyed by subjectKey = payment-installment-reminder:<installmentId>:<dateKey> (reminders.ts:465-474)
flows.side_effect: Same external call + status-update pattern as F010-004
flows.idempotency_key: none (DB constraint) — subjectKey string match against SentMessage.subject at any status in ['prepared','sent','delivered'] (reminders.ts:465-474); same check-then-insert race window as F010-004, though the wider status set makes same-day double-send less likely than F010-004's narrower check
flows.delivery_guarantee_currently_achieved: best-effort, same crash-gap risk as F010-004
flows.pii_health_data_sensitivity: HIGH — patient name, phone, payment amount/installment details
flows.migration_risk: MEDIUM-HIGH — same consent-gate preservation requirement as F010-004; also touches financial/payment context

flows.name: Practitioner daily schedule notification send
flows.initiating_transaction: prisma.setting.upsert() keyed by a clinicId_key unique constraint used as an atomic dedup marker (reminders.ts:377-384, 403-418)
flows.side_effect: WhatsApp send to practitioner with the day's schedule
flows.idempotency_key: Setting.clinicId_key unique constraint — a TRUE DB-level atomic idempotency guard, unlike F010-004/F010-005
flows.delivery_guarantee_currently_achieved: best-effort for the send itself, but duplicate-send prevention is solid (atomic upsert, no race window)
flows.audit_evidence: none beyond ActivityLog pattern shared with the file
flows.pii_health_data_sensitivity: MEDIUM — aggregated schedule data, practitioner-facing not patient-facing
flows.candidate_outbox_reasoning: Already solved correctly with an atomic DB constraint — this is the concrete counter-example proving not every send in this file needs an outbox. Included specifically to demonstrate the inventory does not label every background process as needing a queue, per task instruction.

flows.name: Appointment request creation and conversion → confirmation notification
flows.initiating_transaction: prisma.appointment.create() + prisma.appointmentRequest.update({status:'converted'}), two sequential awaited calls, NOT wrapped in prisma.$transaction (appointmentRequests.ts:281-310)
flows.side_effect: sendAppointmentRequestConfirmationNotification(...).catch(err => console.error(...)) called AFTER res.status(201).json(...) is already sent (appointmentRequests.ts:319-338)
flows.retry_behavior: none — .catch(console.error) is the entire error handling
flows.idempotency_key: none for the notification send; AppointmentRequest.status='converted' blocks re-conversion only if both prior writes already succeeded (:204)
flows.delivery_guarantee_currently_achieved: best-effort, fully decoupled — if the notification fails, the appointment state is already correct/durable, but the patient never learns their appointment was confirmed and nothing retries
flows.failure_state: two distinct failure modes: (a) notification send fails — silent, no patient-facing consequence beyond a missed confirmation; (b) crash between appointment.create and appointmentRequest.update (no $transaction) — leaves Appointment created but AppointmentRequest stuck at 'pending', no compensating action
flows.replay_capability: no dedicated replay for (a); manual staff re-conversion for (b) blocked only if the second write actually succeeded
flows.audit_evidence: ActivityLog for the conversion (appointmentRequests.ts:312-317); no audit event for notification outcome
flows.pii_health_data_sensitivity: HIGH — patient name, phone, appointment type/practitioner
flows.candidate_outbox_reasoning: Two separate gaps: (1) the appointment-create + request-update pair should be wrapped in one $transaction regardless of any outbox decision (a narrower fix, PARTIAL-style, that does not require a queue); (2) the confirmation notification is exactly the 'commit the state change, then reliably deliver a side effect' shape an outbox formalizes.
flows.candidate_queue_reasoning: Notification delivery needs retry/backoff it does not have today.

flows.name: Communication consent/preference mutation and audit write
flows.initiating_transaction: setCommunicationPreference(...) writing PatientCommunicationPreference + an append-only, revision-chained PatientCommunicationConsentEvent (communicationPreferences.ts:348-364)
flows.side_effect: writeAuditLog(...) called immediately after (communicationPreferences.ts:366-376) as a SEPARATE, non-transactional awaited Prisma call
flows.retry_behavior: none — synchronous request/response, client responsible for retry on HTTP error
flows.idempotency_key: revision field per (channel, purpose) chain provides ordering, not duplicate-submit idempotency; no idempotency-key header/param
flows.ordering_requirement: YES, strict — revision desc ordering is documented as authoritative only within one (channel, purpose) chain (communicationPreferences.ts:56-65). Any future async delivery mechanism for this flow would need per-(patientId, channel, purpose) partitioned ordering, not global FIFO.
flows.delivery_guarantee_currently_achieved: not an external-delivery flow; internally, the audit write is fire-and-forget relative to the state mutation — if writeAuditLog fails after the preference write commits, the consent change is durable but silently unaudited
flows.failure_state: if setCommunicationPreference throws, mapped to an HTTP error via CommunicationConsentAdminError (:379-385); if it succeeds but the audit write fails, no rollback and no alert
flows.replay_capability: N/A — direct mutation API, not event-driven
flows.audit_evidence: AuditLog row per mutation (action: communication_consent_<action>, :371), per bulk update (:444), per export (:288); a bounded OperationalEvent-backed audit-summary reader exists (source='communication_consent', schema.prisma:1575-1578)
flows.pii_health_data_sensitivity: MEDIUM-HIGH — consent state itself is not clinical, but evidenceType/notes/hashed IP+UA are captured as legal evidence tied to a named patient
flows.candidate_outbox_reasoning: The state-write-then-audit-write gap is a genuine correctness issue, but the fix that already exists elsewhere in this codebase (writeAuditLogInTx inside a single prisma.$transaction, used by clinicBulkExportPackage.ts) is a narrower, non-queue solution. A full outbox is not needed to make this specific pair atomic; it IS relevant if a future cross-domain consumer (e.g. a channel that must react to consent changes) is added — that would be a genuine outbox use case, but does not exist today.
flows.candidate_queue_reasoning: No external delivery or cross-process consumer exists for this flow today.
flows.migration_risk: HIGH — this flow sits inside the KVKK architecture freeze boundary (KVKK_ARCHITECTURE_FREEZE_BOUNDARY.md §2 row 1-5, §3 item 14 explicitly names 'queue/outbox implementation that rewires current consent/audit flows' as frozen). Any future change here is blocked independent of ADR-006/007 status.

flows.name: Clinic bulk export — asynchronous queued worker (KVKK-HIGH-004)
flows.initiating_transaction: reserveClinicBulkExport(...) creates a ClinicBulkExportArchive row with status='queued' (clinicBulkExport.ts:206-215); response is 202 {jobId, status:'queued'}
flows.side_effect: clinicBulkExportWorker.ts cron (*/1 * * * *) claims queued rows via claimQueuedClinicBulkExportJobs — a guarded per-row updateMany so concurrent replicas never double-claim (documented clinicBulkExportWorker.ts:14-19) — then generates the export
flows.tenant_key: scope.clinicId / scope.organizationId, re-validated by the worker at both claim time and generation time
flows.retry_behavior: crash recovery via lease-expiry sweep — an in-flight 'generating' job whose worker died is picked up by the next surviving replica's lease-expiry sweep (clinicBulkExportWorker.ts:34-36)
flows.idempotency_key: guarded claim (status:'queued'->'generating' updateMany, :951-958) is the exclusivity mechanism; ClinicBulkExportAlreadyRunningError/RateLimitedError prevent duplicate concurrent jobs per clinic
flows.delivery_guarantee_currently_achieved: at-least-once generation, exactly-once download (atomic one-time-token claim, clinicBulkExport.ts:441-449, with a 'confirm file opens before claiming the token' pattern to avoid burning a token on a storage failure)
flows.failure_state: typed failureCode field (LEASE_EXPIRED, FEATURE_DISABLED, SIZE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED, ZIP_INTEGRITY_FAILED, LEASE_LOST, GENERATION_ERROR, WORKER_SHUTDOWN) surfaced to the status endpoint — a genuine terminal/dead-letter state with a reason code, not silent
flows.replay_capability: yes by design — this is the strongest existing queue-like pattern in the codebase
flows.audit_evidence: AuditLog at creation-disabled attempt, step-up failures, and three distinct download outcomes (started/completed/failed), some via writeAuditLogInTx (transactional)
flows.pii_health_data_sensitivity: VERY HIGH — full clinic-wide structured + attachment export
flows.candidate_outbox_reasoning: This IS the reference implementation for what a formalized dispatcher should look like elsewhere (see F010-004/005/007). No gap identified.
flows.candidate_queue_reasoning: Already works correctly on the DB-row-claim pattern without an external queue product; the PoC should study this flow as its primary internal precedent rather than treat it as needing migration.
flows.migration_risk: N/A — recommended as the design template, not a migration target

flows.name: Patient-level privacy export (synchronous)
flows.initiating_transaction: POST /patients/:id/privacy/export and /export-package handled entirely inline in the request handler; no job row created beforehand (patientPrivacy.ts:339-378, 463-497)
flows.side_effect: Direct synchronous generation; the ZIP path has internal generation-lease protection (ExportGenerationInProgressError/ExportLeaseLostError, :499-506) suggesting a lease mechanism exists at the service layer even though the route is synchronous
flows.retry_behavior: none — synchronous HTTP call, client must retry
flows.idempotency_key: one-time download token for the ZIP path (claimExportDownload, :564-568); JSON export path has none (naturally repeatable)
flows.delivery_guarantee_currently_achieved: N/A — direct response, no async delivery
flows.failure_state: HTTP error response; no partial/orphaned state observed given the synchronous model
flows.audit_evidence: AuditLog for create and three-state ZIP download outcome — same pattern as F010-009
flows.pii_health_data_sensitivity: VERY HIGH — full patient record including appointments, payments, treatment cases
flows.candidate_outbox_reasoning: No transactional-delivery gap; this is a synchronous-request-latency and consistency-of-architecture question, not an outbox question.
flows.candidate_queue_reasoning: Structurally surprising: two architecturally different export implementations (this one, synchronous; F010-009, queued) exist for a conceptually similar capability. A future decision (out of scope for this PoC design) should evaluate migrating this flow onto the F010-009 worker pattern for consistency and to bound request latency for large patients — this is a candidate for the EXISTING internal worker pattern, not necessarily a new external queue.
flows.migration_risk: LOW-MEDIUM if migrated onto the existing internal pattern; not itself blocked by KVKK freeze boundary (row 12, STABLE/not in active-path list) but out of scope for F0-010

flows.name: Data retention cleanup job (cross-category anonymize/delete)
flows.initiating_transaction: N/A — scheduled sweep, not triggered by a business transaction
flows.side_effect: Hard-deletes WhatsAppConversationMessage, WhatsAppConversationState, OperationalEvent, MessagingInboundEvent, CommunicationConsentConflictBucket; anonymizes ContactRequest; redacts WhatsAppInboxEntry (dataRetentionCleanupJob.ts:77-263). Never touches Patient/Appointment/Treatment/Payment/Insurance/Attachment/AuditLog/ActivityLog (file header :7-11).
flows.tenant_key: NONE — global, unscoped by organizationId/clinicId (e.g. :80, :98) — operates across the entire multi-tenant DB in one run
flows.retry_behavior: none within a run; a category error is recorded and simply retried on the next scheduled run (daily default)
flows.idempotency_key: N/A — threshold-based WHERE createdAt < X deletes are naturally idempotent; explicitly documented as such (file header :14)
flows.ordering_requirement: none — categories run sequentially but are independent
flows.replay_capability: N/A — idempotent by construction, safe to re-run
flows.audit_evidence: none (by design — this job DELETES OperationalEvent rows, it does not write them)
flows.pii_health_data_sensitivity: HIGH — entire purpose is PII lifecycle management
flows.candidate_outbox_reasoning: No transactional-publication need; this is a scheduled batch sweep, correctly idempotent already.
flows.candidate_queue_reasoning: Cron+JobLock is an adequate fit for a global, idempotent, non-latency-sensitive batch job.
flows.migration_risk: N/A — not a migration candidate; flagged instead as an ordering dependency: this job deletes MessagingInboundEvent rows, the SAME table F010-001/002/003's idempotency ledger and retry window rely on — any future outbox/queue design must keep RETRY_WINDOW_MS (6h) safely inside this job's retention threshold, a coupling the PoC must document, not just the job itself.

flows.name: Meta WhatsApp template sync (external status polling)
flows.initiating_transaction: N/A — polls the Meta Graph API for pending template approval status
flows.tenant_key: iterates per-clinicId within a global cron
flows.retry_behavior: none — per-template failure increments summary.failed and continues (:195-203)
flows.idempotency_key: N/A — pure read-then-conditional-update of external status, safe to re-run
flows.delivery_guarantee_currently_achieved: N/A — not a delivery flow, a status-polling flow
flows.pii_health_data_sensitivity: LOW — template metadata only, no patient data
flows.candidate_queue_reasoning: Polling an external status API on a schedule is an appropriate fit for cron; a queue would not change the fundamental need to poll Meta.

flows.name: Inbound event retry job (internal retry/recovery sweep)
flows.side_effect: Re-processes MessagingInboundEvent rows in status='failed' (attempts < 3) for channel='whatsapp', provider='meta_cloud_api' only, oldest-first
flows.tenant_key: N/A — infrastructure-level, operates across all tenants' failed events for the covered provider
flows.retry_behavior: IS the retry mechanism for F010-002 — see that row
flows.idempotency_key: reprocessing is safe because it goes through the same createInboundEventOrDetectDuplicate-adjacent processing path that governs F010-001/002/003
flows.delivery_guarantee_currently_achieved: N/A — this job IS the delivery-guarantee mechanism for the flows it covers
flows.failure_state: stuck 'processing' rows older than STUCK_PROCESSING_MS=1h are self-healed back into the retry pool (:38-48)
flows.replay_capability: this job is itself the closest thing to a replay/DLQ mechanism the codebase has today
flows.pii_health_data_sensitivity: N/A — operates on event metadata, not a new PII surface beyond what F010-001/002/003 already expose
flows.candidate_outbox_reasoning: N/A — this is itself a hand-rolled precursor to part of what a queue/DLQ would provide.
flows.candidate_queue_reasoning: This job is the single clearest 'if/when a queue exists, this logic should move into it' candidate: fixed (not exponential) backoff, no explicit poison-message status, and provider-incomplete coverage (F010-001/003 gap) are all things a real queue/consumer model would generalize. It is also directly relevant to R-034 (duplicate job registration risk) since it shares the JobLock primitive with every other cron job.
flows.migration_risk: MEDIUM — this job's provider-exclusion list (Evolution/Instagram not covered) is the most direct, scoped fix available even without any queue decision; a future task could close F010-001/003's gap by simply widening this job's provider list, independent of any outbox/queue platform choice

flows.name: Imaging bridge agent offline detection
flows.side_effect: prisma.imagingBridgeAgent.updateMany() marking stale agents status='offline' (OFFLINE_THRESHOLD_MINUTES default 5, :18, :40)
flows.tenant_key: agent paired to clinic/org at pairing time (imagingBridgePublic.ts:399, 464)
flows.retry_behavior: N/A — pure state sweep, no external call
flows.idempotency_key: N/A — updateMany on a status+time filter is naturally idempotent
flows.failure_state: N/A — no external calls to fail
flows.audit_evidence: none observed for the sweep itself
flows.pii_health_data_sensitivity: LOW — agent connectivity status only, no imaging PHI
flows.candidate_queue_reasoning: Simple heartbeat-timeout housekeeping; cron is an appropriate fit.

flows.name: Imaging bridge study upload (synchronous push)
flows.initiating_transaction: POST /imaging/bridge/studies (:199), synchronous push from the on-prem bridge agent
flows.side_effect: Not fully traced in this bounded investigation (400+ line handler not read in full)
flows.retry_behavior: AMBIGUOUS — not confirmed in this pass
flows.idempotency_key: AMBIGUOUS — not confirmed in this pass
flows.audit_evidence: AMBIGUOUS — an 'imaging_bridge_heartbeat_first_seen' audit-style log was found for the heartbeat path (:184), not confirmed for the studies-upload path specifically
flows.pii_health_data_sensitivity: VERY HIGH if confirmed synchronous — imaging PHI (DICOM/CBCT study data) transferred inline in an HTTP request
flows.candidate_outbox_reasoning: Large synchronous PHI upload is a plausible latency/reliability concern, but no evidence of an actual problem (timeout, failure rate) was gathered in this bounded pass — flagged for a future targeted investigation rather than treated as a PoC input.
flows.candidate_queue_reasoning: Same reasoning — do not classify without deeper evidence.

flows.name: Public booking notice evidence cleanup job
flows.side_effect: Deletes orphaned PublicBookingNoticeEvidence rows never linked to a booking

flows.name: Clinic bulk export cleanup job
flows.side_effect: Sweeps expired export archives, stale password attempts, orphaned partial files (filesystem + DB)
flows.tenant_key: N/A — global sweep across all clinics' archives
flows.pii_health_data_sensitivity: MEDIUM — operates on already-exported archive metadata/files, not live patient records

flows.name: In-app notification generation (pull-based / materialized on read)
flows.initiating_transaction: None — no createNotification() call exists anywhere triggered by the underlying business event. GET /api/notifications instead LAZILY computes and upserts Notification rows on every dashboard poll by querying live Appointment/Task/AppointmentRequest/LabWorkOrder tables for current-state matches (notifications.ts:96-104, 123-131, 147-155, 181-188).
flows.side_effect: prisma.notification.upsert() keyed by @@unique([clinicId, externalId]) (schema.prisma:1269), called from within a GET (read) request handler — a read endpoint with a side-effecting write
flows.retry_behavior: N/A — recomputed idempotently on every poll
flows.idempotency_key: @@unique([clinicId, externalId]) genuinely prevents duplicate rows across repeated polls; isRead state preserved across re-upserts (:36-40)
flows.delivery_guarantee_currently_achieved: N/A in the traditional sense — but 'freshness' guarantee is weak: a notification only materializes when SOME user with clinic access loads the notifications endpoint. If no one opens the app, upcoming_appointment notifications may never materialize in their intended lead-time window.
flows.failure_state: N/A — no explicit failure path; the failure mode is staleness, not error
flows.audit_evidence: none — UI plumbing, not an audited event stream
flows.pii_health_data_sensitivity: MEDIUM — title/subtitle embed patient full names (notifications.ts:99) and task/lab descriptions
flows.candidate_outbox_reasoning: The single clearest 'textbook' outbox use case found in this inventory: reacting to a committed business-state transition (appointment created, task due, lab order ready) to materialize a derived read-model row is exactly what an outbox+consumer replaces a stale polling-upsert-on-read pattern with. Also flagged: Notification rows are NEVER cleaned up — not present in dataRetentionCleanupJob.ts's category list (F010-011) — an unbounded-growth risk independent of any outbox decision.
flows.candidate_queue_reasoning: An outbox-driven consumer that creates Notification rows on commit does not by itself require an external queue — it could be a synchronous in-process listener within the same transaction/request-adjacent code. A queue becomes relevant only if notification creation needs to be decoupled from the originating request's latency budget.
flows.migration_risk: MEDIUM — touches four different originating domains (Appointments, Tasks, Appointment Requests, Lab Work Orders), each of which would need an emission point

infrastructure_primitives.name: JobLock — DB-row lease lock
infrastructure_primitives.mechanism: Postgres-table-based lease lock, NOT Postgres advisory locks, NOT Redis (Redis SET NX PX was considered and explicitly rejected in a code comment to avoid extra infrastructure, jobLock.ts:15-17). acquireJobLock claims an expired lock via a guarded updateMany or creates a fresh row (P2002 on race); releaseJobLock only clears a lock it still owns; withJobLock wraps acquire->run->release-in-finally.
infrastructure_primitives.crash_behavior: If a process crashes while holding the lock, nothing releases it — the lease simply expires at lockedUntil and the next tick's acquireJobLock claims it. Purely lease-expiry-based, no explicit crash detection.
infrastructure_primitives.coverage: Used by 8 of 9 job files (reminders, dataRetentionCleanup, metaTemplateSync, inboundEventRetry, imagingBridgeOffline, publicBookingNoticeEvidenceCleanup, patientPrivacyExportCleanup, clinicBulkExportCleanup). clinicBulkExportWorker.ts deliberately opts OUT in favor of its own per-row guarded-claim pattern to allow multiple concurrent replicas (documented, clinicBulkExportWorker.ts:5-13) — JobLock is a mutual-exclusion primitive (one worker at a time), not a work-distribution primitive (many workers sharing a queue).
infrastructure_primitives.relevance_to_poc: This is the closest existing analogue to a queue-claim mechanism (leasing, SKIP-LOCKED-adjacent semantics via guarded updateMany) and should be the PoC's baseline comparison point for 'claim safely' experiments (see f0-010-poc-test-matrix.md Experiments 3-4).

infrastructure_primitives.name: OperationalEvent — dedup-keyed operational event table
infrastructure_primitives.mechanism: id, organizationId, clinicId?, severity(info|warning|error|critical), source(whatsapp|appointment|finance|auth|system), message, metadata (Json), resolvedAt?, resolvedById?, createdAt, dedupeKey? @unique. Doc comment explicitly frames dedupeKey as enabling atomic upsert-based dedup ('never check-then-insert', schema.prisma:1566-1568).
infrastructure_primitives.current_usage: Written by clinicBulkExportPackage.ts (F010-009); read by communicationConsentAuditReport.ts (F010-008's audit-summary endpoint). recordOperationalEvent() (operationalEventService.ts:32-47) is a simple wrap-and-swallow writer with NO callers found in the webhook/reminder/appointment-request code paths (F010-001 through F010-007) — an apparently underused mechanism relative to its design intent.
infrastructure_primitives.relevance_to_poc: Has a dedup key and a metadata JSON payload — structurally the closest existing thing to an outbox row in the schema today, but lacks any processing-status field (pending/dispatched/delivered) an actual outbox would need. A future outbox design should explicitly decide whether to extend this table or introduce a new one, rather than silently duplicating the concept (this ambiguity risk is analogous to the two-mechanism overlap already flagged as R-047 for consent-audit concepts).

infrastructure_primitives.name: AuditLog — two-tier transactional/non-transactional write pattern
infrastructure_primitives.mechanism: writeAuditLog() (auditLog.ts:37-58) is fire-and-forget, swallows all internal errors ('logging must never crash the main operation'), and at most call sites (e.g. appointments.ts:392, communicationPreferences.ts:366) is invoked WITHOUT await — genuinely detached from the request/response lifecycle. writeAuditLogInTx() (auditLog.ts:78-97) requires a Prisma.TransactionClient, does NOT swallow errors, and is used ONLY by clinicBulkExportPackage.ts for a deliberately small allowlist of fail-closed security-critical events (export request creation, download-token issuance, download claim/start).
infrastructure_primitives.relevance_to_poc: The codebase already has, in miniature, the exact two-tier pattern an outbox design would formalize at scale: most writes are best-effort/decoupled today (no durability guarantee across a crash between the state change and the audit write — see F010-007, F010-008), and a handful of security-critical ones are already pulled into the same transaction as the state change. This is direct repository evidence that the narrower fix ('use writeAuditLogInTx more widely') is available and does not by itself require an outbox or queue — it should be evaluated as an alternative to, or a complement to, F0-010's PoC before assuming a full outbox is the only path to closing these gaps.

counts.note: Counts cover the 18 business flows only, not the 3 infrastructure primitives (which are not classified for outbox/queue need — they ARE the primitives a future outbox/queue would build on or replace).
